meta.description: 100 lightweight multi-agent benchmark tasks (2-3 agents each) for AgentGlue coordination and cache evaluation
meta.design: 10 groups of 10 tasks testing different multi-agent coordination patterns. Each task spawns 2-3 sub-agents with overlapping file reads and searches.

tasks.name: Runtime public API vs private helpers
tasks.description: 2 agents read runtime.py — one lists public methods, the other lists private helpers
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all public methods (no leading underscore) with their signatures.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all private/internal methods (leading underscore) with their purpose.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read runtime.py; second agent should get cache hit.

tasks.name: Dedup class hierarchy vs error handling
tasks.description: 2 agents read dedup.py — one maps class hierarchy, the other checks error handling
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and map the class hierarchy (which classes inherit from what).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and list all try/except blocks and what errors they handle.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read dedup.py.

tasks.name: Shared memory read vs write paths
tasks.description: 2 agents read shared_memory.py — one traces read path, the other traces write path
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and trace the data read path from entry to return.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and trace the data write path from entry to storage.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read shared_memory.py.

tasks.name: Task lock concurrency vs API surface
tasks.description: 2 agents read task_lock.py — one analyzes concurrency, the other lists the API
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and identify all concurrency primitives (locks, semaphores, events) used.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and list every public method with its parameters and return type.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read task_lock.py.

tasks.name: Allocator resource management vs algorithm
tasks.description: 2 agents read allocator.py — one checks resource management, the other describes the allocation algorithm
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and identify how resources are acquired and released.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and describe the allocation algorithm step by step.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read allocator.py.

tasks.name: Events data structures vs dispatch logic
tasks.description: 2 agents read events.py — one lists data structures, the other describes dispatch
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/events.py and list all data classes, enums, and type aliases defined.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/events.py and explain the event dispatch mechanism.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read events.py.

tasks.name: Metrics collection vs reporting
tasks.description: 2 agents read metrics.py — one analyzes collection, the other analyzes reporting
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/metrics.py and describe how metrics are collected and stored.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/metrics.py and describe how metrics are reported or exported.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read metrics.py.

tasks.name: Recorder storage format vs replay capability

Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:

- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/recorder.py and determine whether replay/playback capability exists and how it works.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read recorder.py.

tasks.name: Sidecar server endpoints vs middleware chain

tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:

- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/sidecar/server.py and trace the middleware/handler chain from request to response.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read server.py.

- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/src/index.ts and list all configuration options and their defaults.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read index.ts.

tasks.name: Search 'async' in middleware vs full src
tasks.description: 2 agents search for 'async' — one in middleware/, one in all of src/
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for the pattern 'async' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/ and count occurrences per file.
- Agent 2: Search for the pattern 'async' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/ and count occurrences per file.
Report combined findings noting the overlap.
tasks.notes: middleware/ is inside src/agentglue/, so agent 2's search is a superset of agent 1's.

tasks.name: Search 'import' in core vs full src

- Agent 2: Search for 'import' statements in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/ and list all external dependencies.
Report combined findings noting the overlap.
tasks.notes: core/ is inside src/agentglue/, so results overlap.

Report combined findings.

tasks.name: Dedup security vs performance review
tasks.description: 2 agents review dedup.py — one for security, one for performance
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and review it for security issues (SQL injection, path traversal, unsafe deserialization).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and review it for performance issues (unnecessary copies, O(n^2) loops, blocking I/O).
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read the same file from different angles.

tasks.name: Shared memory security vs performance review
tasks.description: 2 agents review shared_memory.py — one for security, one for performance
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and review for security issues (race conditions, data leaks, unauthorized access).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and review for performance issues (memory waste, slow lookups, excessive locking).
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read shared_memory.py.

tasks.name: Task lock security vs performance review

tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:

- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and review for performance issues (contention, unnecessary lock scope, blocking waits).
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read task_lock.py.

tasks.prompt:

tasks.name: Sidecar server security vs reliability review
